general: Increase energy-dense, nutrient-rich foods (nuts, dairy, lean protein, whole grains).
Eat 5–6 smaller meals per day and add healthy snacks.
Include strength training 2–3x/week to build lean mass.

age_notes.child: Consult pediatric guidance; growth patterns vary. Ensure adequate protein, calcium, vitamin D.
age_notes.adult: Screen for underlying causes (thyroid, malabsorption) if persistent.
age_notes.older: Monitor sarcopenia risk; prioritize protein and resistance exercise.

job_notes.sedentary: Set reminders to eat regularly; add calorie-dense snacks.
job_notes.moderate: Balance activity with higher protein/calorie intake.
job_notes.active: Increase post-activity recovery meals with carbs + protein.

general: Maintain balanced diet (vegetables, fruits, whole grains, lean proteins, healthy fats).
Aim for 150–300 min/wk moderate activity or 75–150 min/wk vigorous, plus 2+ days muscle-strengthening.

age_notes.child: Encourage play and varied activities daily.
age_notes.adult: Keep regular screenings and balanced routines.
age_notes.older: Add balance/flexibility work to reduce fall risk.

job_notes.sedentary: Stand or walk short breaks each hour.

general: Create a modest calorie deficit via diet quality and portion control.
Target ≥150–300 min/wk moderate aerobic activity; include 2–3 days resistance training.
Limit sugary drinks and ultra-processed foods; prioritize fiber and protein.

age_notes.child: Focus on family-based lifestyle habits; avoid restrictive dieting without supervision.
age_notes.adult: Combine diet changes with consistent activity; track habits.
age_notes.older: Protect muscle with protein + strength training; discuss safe goals with a clinician.

job_notes.sedentary: Schedule walking meetings or micro-breaks; use a step goal.
job_notes.moderate: Leverage routine activity; refine diet and strength work.
